notes: Agent self-reports task progress via an in-band hidden marker (irrlicht-eta) driven by the FENCED instruction-file rule seeded into <cwd>/AGENTS.md. The pi parser now scans full message.content[] text blocks via the shared tailer.ScanTaskEstimate (port landed 1ae457d9, parser.go:88-107) and sets ev.TaskEstimate; the tailer persists it, the domain projects task_completion_eta (ForecastTaskCompletion), and the API surfaces both — the full pipeline is wired for pi. The PHASE DSL asserts only the user-observable lifecycle arc — a multi-turn ready→working→ready walk to a ready finish; the estimate-content invariants (completed_rounds present + non-decreasing + reaching total_rounds; updated_at advancing; ETA present once ≥1 round reported and burning down) live in the recipe verify[] and are covered by the per-adapter parser unit tests, mirroring how token-accounting's monotonic-token invariant lives outside the phase form. The interactive recipe runs 4 driven turns so completed_rounds climbs 0/4→4/4; the session must outlive the final turn by ≥10s so the post-turn settle to ready is captured. NOTE: route is driver-gap — record must port a seed_instruction step into the pi driver (no reference driver has it; same gap as the opencode cell) before driving. Emission is model-discretion (soft): record settles whether pi actually emits a parseable marker. observations assert pi as the agent + nonzero cost/tokens over the run.

text: Interactive pi REPL launches in the seeded workspace and appears ready
First prompt (plan + emit 0/4 marker) flips the session to working
First turn completes (initial marker emitted) → back to ready
A later driven turn (advancing toward 4/4) flips back to working
Task finishes (4/4) and the session settles to ready for ≥10s; state never entered waiting — final state ready
pi REPL exits at end of run — process_exited on the session
